proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Sports Medicine Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite sports medicine model linking knee, shoulder, elbow, and foot-ankle regional hubs.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Sports Knee Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Knee ligament, meniscus, extensor, and articular structures for sports knee conditions.
Primary medial knee stabilizer resisting valgus stress.
Primary lateral knee stabilizer resisting varus stress.
Posterolateral knee stabilizing complex including LCL, popliteus, and arcuate ligaments.
C-shaped medial meniscus distributing load and resisting shear at the medial compartment.
More mobile lateral meniscus with popliteus hiatus; commonly torn with ACL injuries.
Hyaline cartilage covering femoral condyles and tibial plateau.
Medial and lateral femoral condyles forming the proximal tibiofemoral articulation.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Sports Shoulder Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Glenohumeral stabilizers, rotator cuff, and biceps anchor for shoulder sports conditions.
Scapular articular surface forming the socket of the glenohumeral joint.
Fibrocartilaginous rim deepening the glenoid and anchoring capsulolabral structures.
Supraspinatus, infraspinatus, subscapularis, and teres minor force-couple complex.
Superior rotator cuff tendon initiating abduction and commonly torn in cuff pathology.
Posterior cuff tendon providing external rotation strength.
Anterior cuff tendon providing internal rotation and anterior stability.
Inferior posterior cuff muscle contributing to external rotation.
Superior labrum and biceps anchor complex at the glenoid origin.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Sports Elbow Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Elbow ligament and tendon structures relevant to throwing and flexion injuries.
Medial elbow stabilizer critical in throwing athletes.
Lateral elbow stabilizer resisting varus stress.
Distal biceps insertion on the radial tuberosity enabling elbow flexion and supination.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Sports Foot & Ankle Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Lateral ankle ligaments, syndesmosis, Achilles, and hindfoot structures for sports foot-ankle conditions.
Primary lateral ankle stabilizer injured in inversion sprains.
Lateral ankle ligament stabilizing subtalar motion after inversion injury.
Conjoined gastrocnemius-soleus tendon inserting on the calcaneus.
Cross-cluster foot-ankle anatomy reference for sports medicine (talus).
Cross-cluster foot-ankle anatomy reference for sports medicine (calcaneus).
Cross-cluster foot-ankle anatomy reference for sports medicine (syndesmosis).
Cross-cluster foot-ankle anatomy reference for sports medicine (deltoid-ligament).
Cross-cluster foot-ankle anatomy reference for sports medicine (lateral-malleolus).
Cross-cluster foot-ankle anatomy reference for sports medicine (medial-malleolus).
Syndesmotic high ankle sprain.
Educational classification framework for syndesmotic sprain.

metadata.claim_text: Syndesmotic sprains cause prolonged recovery when missed; external rotation and squeeze tests raise suspicion.
Syndesmotic Sprain pathoanatomy centers on syndesmosis, talus, deltoid-ligament and related stabilizers.
Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating syndesmotic sprain.
Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for syndesmotic sprain — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.
State mechanism, key exam findings, imaging role, treatment concept, and return-to-play framework for syndesmotic sprain.
Rehabilitation milestones and return-to-play criteria for syndesmotic sprain require attending-defined progression gates.
Weber B/C ankle fracture patterns and syndesmotic injury share instability language — evaluate mortise and syndesmosis integrity.

metadata.trigger: Active athlete with functional instability or mechanical symptoms from syndesmotic sprain despite appropriate initial management
metadata.action: Discuss operative reconstruction versus continued rehabilitation; graft selection and fixation are attending decisions

metadata.trigger: Athlete approaching return-to-sport after syndesmotic sprain treatment
metadata.action: Apply sport-specific return-to-play criteria with strength, stability, and functional testing milestones
